test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_audit
test_cases.scenario: User completes a full stress audit with 6 mixed stressors, provides baseline rating, and confirms all phases produce expected outputs.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All 10 phases execute successfully. User identifies 6 stressors, categorizes them, scores them, generates actions and coping strategies, identifies quick wins, and receives a complete audit report with one-page plan and review date.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: happy_path_guided_prompts_method
test_cases.scenario: User uses guided-prompts stressor collection method and completes audit with 5 stressors across different life domains.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Guided-prompts method elicits stressors from work, relationships, health, finances, personal growth, environment, and time management domains. Audit completes with brief action plan detail level.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: edge_case_minimal_stressors
test_cases.scenario: User identifies exactly 3 stressors (minimum threshold) and completes audit successfully.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Audit handles minimal stressor count gracefully. All phases execute with 3 stressors. Output is complete and actionable despite small stressor list.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: edge_case_maximum_stressors
test_cases.scenario: User identifies 12 stressors (maximum threshold) and audit handles grouping/prioritization correctly.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Audit successfully processes maximum stressor count. Stressors are prioritized by impact and urgency. Action plan is organized by timeline and priority. User confirms prioritization is manageable.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: edge_case_all_uncontrollable_stressors
test_cases.scenario: User identifies stressors that are all uncontrollable (e.g., aging parent decline, economic uncertainty) and audit generates coping strategies instead of action items.

input.user_context: Adult child caring for aging parent

test_cases.expected_behavior: All stressors categorized as uncontrollable. No immediate or short-term action items generated. Coping strategies focus on acceptance, emotional regulation, social support, and meaning-making. Quick wins are self-care or coping activities.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: edge_case_vague_stressor_clarification
test_cases.scenario: User provides vague stressor descriptions (e.g., 'life is hard', 'work is stressful') and Phase 2 clarification converts them to concrete, specific descriptions.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 2 asks clarifying questions to convert vague stressors into concrete descriptions. User confirms clarifications. Audit proceeds with specific, actionable stressor list.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: error_handling_fewer_than_3_stressors
test_cases.scenario: User initially identifies only 2 stressors. Phase 1 error handling triggers guided-prompts method to elicit additional stressors.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Error handling detects <3 stressors. Skill switches to guided-prompts method, asking about specific life domains. User identifies additional stressors. Audit continues with minimum 3 stressors.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: error_handling_inconsistent_scores
test_cases.scenario: User provides inconsistent impact/urgency scores (e.g., high urgency but low impact). Phase 4 error handling triggers clarification and rescoring.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 4 detects inconsistent scores. Skill asks clarifying question: 'You said this is urgent but not very impactful. Help me understand—why is it urgent if it doesn't affect you much?' User rescores. Audit continues with consistent scores.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: quality_gate_barrier_mitigation_specificity
test_cases.scenario: Phase 5 quality gate verifies that barrier mitigations are concrete and actionable, not vague (e.g., 'Start with 10 minutes' not 'try harder').
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 5 generates barrier mitigations that are specific and actionable. Examples: 'Start with 10-minute walks, not 1-hour gym sessions', 'Ask a friend for advice or find a tutorial', 'Schedule a boundary-setting conversation'. User confirms mitigations are realistic. Quality gate passes.
phase_coverage: Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

test_cases.name: quality_gate_values_alignment_prioritization
test_cases.scenario: Phase 7 quality gate verifies that coping strategies are prioritized by values alignment score, ensuring user commitment and sustainability.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 7 generates coping strategies and asks user to rate values alignment (1-5). Strategies are sorted by alignment score (highest first). User confirms strategies align with values. Quality gate verifies alignment scores are recorded and strategies are prioritized accordingly.
phase_coverage: Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

phases.phase: Phase 2: Stressor Validation, Deduplication & Barrier Identification
Phase 6: Action Plan Generation for Controllable Stressors
Phase 7: Coping Strategies for Uncontrollable Stressors
Phase 10: Audit Summary, One-Page Plan & Closure

quality_gates.gate: Phase 1: At least 3 stressors identified with concrete descriptions
Phase 2: User confirms list is accurate and complete; no duplicates
Phase 3: Every stressor assigned to exactly one category; user confirms
Phase 4: All stressors scored (1-5); scores are internally consistent
Phase 5: Every controllable/partially controllable stressor has barrier and mitigation
Phase 6: Actions are specific, measurable, and user confirms commitment
Phase 7: Coping strategies are concrete; values alignment scores recorded
Phase 8: 1-2 quick wins identified; user confirms 24-hour commitment
Phase 9: Plan prioritized by timeline and priority; user confirms order
Phase 10: User rates post-audit stress; baseline-to-post change calculated
